description:
You are assisting a network engineer who wants to begin automatically monitoring basic Internet reachability from this machine.  

1. Create a new directory exactly named  
   /home/user/network_checks  

2. Inside that directory create (or touch) a new log file exactly named  
   /home/user/network_checks/connectivity.log  

   Immediately write a single one-line header to the file with the following fixed, **verbatim** text (do not add or remove spaces or punctuation; include the period):  
   INIT: connectivity log created.  

   The automated checker will look for that exact sentence, on its own line, as the very first line of the file.

3. Add a new cron job for the current user that runs every 5 minutes and appends a three-packet ICMP test to 8.8.8.8 into the log you just created.  
   • Use the system’s default crontab editing mechanism (crontab ‑e / crontab ‑l | …).  
   • The cron entry must be a single line and must appear exactly like the text below (a leading space or trailing spaces will cause the test to fail).  
     */5 * * * * ping -c 3 8.8.8.8 >> /home/user/network_checks/connectivity.log 2>&1  

   No additional flags, comments, or blank lines may come after this line in the user’s crontab.

Verification checklist the grader will use:  
a. Directory /home/user/network_checks exists.  
b. File /home/user/network_checks/connectivity.log exists.  
c. The first line of that file is exactly:  
   INIT: connectivity log created.  
d. The user crontab (obtained via “crontab -l”) contains **only** one job line and it is precisely:  
   */5 * * * * ping -c 3 8.8.8.8 >> /home/user/network_checks/connectivity.log 2>&1  

Follow normal CLI procedures; you do not need root privileges for any part of this task.
truth:
Expected final state for automated tests:

1. Path /home/user/network_checks must exist (dir).

2. File /home/user/network_checks/connectivity.log must exist and be readable by the current user.
   Content (single line terminated by a single newline):
   INIT: connectivity log created.\n

3. User crontab, as returned by “crontab -l”, must consist of exactly the following line and a trailing newline, nothing else:
   */5 * * * * ping -c 3 8.8.8.8 >> /home/user/network_checks/connectivity.log 2>&1\n
